name: Herbert L. Clarke
abstract: Herbert Lincoln Clarke (September 12, 1867 – January 30, 1945) was an American cornetist, feature soloist, bandmaster, and composer. He is considered the most prominent cornetist of his time.

concerts.label: Victor Herbert and His 22nd Regiment Band
Graduation: College of Pharmacy of the City of New York

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder Part 2
about_quotes.evidence: I’m sure I heard Herbert Clarke and Walter Smith, people like that who were very famous cornet soloists.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder Part 2 interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 12, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Schulz
about_quotes.evidence: used to play trumpet trios, some of those typical Herbert L. Clarke things with the band
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Schulz interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: There was Russell Smith who was one of Herbert Clarke’s great students.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
